uses: Lithocardium glabratum, also known as Lithospermum glabratum, is a plant species that has been found to have various medicinal uses, although limited research is available. Historically, it has been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions such as fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's roots and leaves contain compounds with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's benefits and potential applications. Its traditional uses have been documented in various regions, but modern scientific validation is lacking.
